meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Non-Coders: A Founder’s Case Study
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing without writing code. Learn how to launch profitable products using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led beginner case study.
content.answer_block: For non-technical founders, pricing isn't a math problem—it's a value-delivery mechanism. By using Antigravity to scaffold your MVP and Claude Code to iterate on logic, you can implement dynamic, tier-based pricing models that convert users while bypassing the need to write complex backend infrastructure from scratch.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a code editor is the graveyard of a thousand SaaS dreams. You have the vision for a high-converting product, but the barrier of 'no coding experience' feels like a concrete wall between you and your first dollar. The market doesn't care about your syntax; it cares about the value you provide.

We are shifting the paradigm. By leveraging AI-native tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, we bridge the gap between business logic and functional software. You aren't just building an app; you are architecting a revenue engine. Let's strip away the technical complexity and focus on what actually moves the needle: pricing architecture that drives recurring revenue.

steps.title: Defining Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, define what features trigger an upgrade. Focus on usage limits and specialized access to drive conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a SaaS pricing schema with three tiers: Free (limited), Pro (standard), and Enterprise (custom). Include fields for monthly_price, annual_discount, and feature_flag_access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate a pricing model schema based on the provided JSON structure'

steps.title: Scaffolding the Payment Gateway
steps.description: Integration is where most founders fail. We use AI to map your pricing logic to Stripe's API structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the backend boilerplate to integrate Stripe Checkout sessions with the pricing tiers defined in step 1.

steps.title: Implementing Dynamic Pricing Logic
steps.description: The code needs to handle user state. If a user is on a 'Free' tier, restrict access to premium endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function that checks the user's active subscription tier before granting access to the protected API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file auth_middleware.js --prompt 'add a check for active subscription tier'

steps.title: Building the Pricing Dashboard
steps.description: The UI is your primary sales asset. It must be clean, high-converting, and responsive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean React pricing component that displays the three tiers, highlights the 'Pro' plan, and triggers the Stripe checkout flow.

steps.title: Testing Subscription Flows
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate edge cases, such as card declines or subscription cancellations, to ensure your revenue is protected.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that simulates user subscription upgrades and downgrades to ensure the billing state updates correctly.

pro_tips: Always price against the value of the problem you solve, not the cost of your server overhead.
Implement a 'hidden' annual discount toggle; it typically boosts conversion by 15-20% immediately.
Use Claude Code to auto-generate unit tests for your pricing logic; billing bugs are the fastest way to lose customer trust.
Avoid 'feature creep' in your tiers; keep your entry-level plan focused on a single 'Aha!' moment.
Use Antigravity to map your database schema before touching UI components to ensure billing data is immutable.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment, run a 'smoke test' on the checkout flow with Stripe test cards, and then move to your first 10 beta users to validate the price point.
content.conversion_block: The only difference between a hobbyist and a founder is the willingness to ship. Your lack of coding experience is now your greatest asset because it forces you to use the most efficient tools available. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
